meta.description: Same as chaotic_party_game but with AI-personalized missions that reference player names, recent events, and inside jokes. Compare results with chaotic_party_game to measure AI impact.

votingMechanic.name: Unanimous or Bust
votingMechanic.description: ONE bullshit call and it's over. All or nothing.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.dialogue: Oh dude, YES! Pat, I literally saw that—they were totally scrolling like thirty seconds ago. We're giving this one!
jade.internal_thought: This is exactly why I love game night, everyone's already hunting for points and it's only been three minutes—let's GO.

pat.dialogue: Whoa whoa, hold on—I literally just claimed that on myself like thirty seconds ago as a test run. We haven't even really started yet, everyone's still settling in. This doesn't count.
pat.internal_thought: I was literally just making sure the claim system would fire properly and now I'm burning credibility three minutes in by having to defend my own test claim; this is awkward and kills the momentum before we even have any.

sam.dialogue: Um... yeah, I mean, I didn't see that happen, but... Pat saw it, right? I guess that counts?
sam.internal_thought: Wait, was someone on their phone? I wasn't paying attention. Pat seems confident though and nobody else is arguing, so probably fine.

marcus.dialogue: Did you actually see Sam on their phone or are you just fishing? Because if you're guessing, I'm calling bullshit.
marcus.internal_thought: It's early and Pat's hunting easy points, but I need to know if this is legit before I vote — one bad claim now sets a precedent for sloppy gameplay all night.

timeline.title: Who would be the best spy?
timeline.description: Who would be the best spy?
timeline.pollQuestion: Who would be the best spy?

alex.dialogue: Oh, this is perfect — obviously me, but let's see who actually votes for themselves because that's the real tell. Voting for Pat, by the way.
alex.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of stupid question that creates drama — everyone's gonna vote strategically or ego-vote, and I'm gonna watch who reveals themselves, plus I can seed some paranoia about who people think is sneaky.

jade.dialogue: Oh MY god, okay okay—Marcus is absolutely the best spy, no question. He's got that calm vibe that makes you think he's harmless but he's like, watching EVERYTHING. Plus he'd never crack under pressure!
jade.internal_thought: This is PERFECT—we get to talk about each other and vote on people? This is literally the energy I came here for, let's hype everyone up.

pat.dialogue: Ooh, okay okay—Jade's definitely got the spy energy, right? But honestly, Marcus is the quiet type; nobody ever suspects the quiet ones.
pat.internal_thought: Good timing for a poll—light, fun, gets everyone thinking about each other. Keeps the vibe moving without stakes. Jade's clearly the 'right' answer but I might vote Marcus to mix it up and start some friendly debate.

tyler.dialogue: Oh, uh... spy? Like, I dunno, probably Alex or something. Alex seems sneaky.
tyler.internal_thought: This is fun but also I should probably check if my fantasy league trade went through... wait, am I voting right now?

sam.dialogue: Oh man, um... *laughs a beat late* ...I don't know, that's tough. I think maybe Alex? They seem pretty calculated.
sam.internal_thought: Okay this is fun, I'm being asked my opinion directly on something easy — I can do this without looking stupid, just pick someone and commit.

marcus.dialogue: Best spy? I don't know, probably Jade since she's observant. Can we actually start the game now?
marcus.internal_thought: This poll is pointless filler that's stalling setup—I came to win the actual game, not answer dumb hypotheticals.

timeline.title: Freeze!
timeline.description: Everyone freeze in place for 10 seconds. Last one to move wins 5 bonus points. Anyone who moves first loses 2.

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.dialogue: Oh THIS is it — this is what I'm talking about. Everyone lock up, let's see who cracks first.
alex.internal_thought: Perfect timing, perfect chaos fuel — I'm already watching everyone like a hawk to see who twitches, and I'm absolutely going to fake-out at the last second to psych people out.

pat.dialogue: Okay, okay—freeze! Everyone lock it in right NOW. This is peak chaos energy, I love it.
pat.internal_thought: Perfect timing—we're still in setup, everyone's loose and laughing, and Marcus needs something to pull him back in; this freeze game is exactly the kind of goofy physical moment that resets the vibe.

sam.dialogue: Oh! Okay, okay—*immediately locks into position with a slight grin* This is fun, I like this one.
sam.internal_thought: This is easy and nobody's watching me mess up—I can actually win something here if I just stay still.

marcus.dialogue: Seriously? We're doing freeze tag now? Can we please just play the actual game?
marcus.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of stupid filler nonsense that derails the match — we're barely into setup and they're already throwing random events at us instead of letting the game breathe.

votingMechanic.name: Pitch It
votingMechanic.description: The claimant gets 15 seconds to make their case. Then you vote.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.dialogue: Ooh Pat, okay okay—lay it on us! What'd you catch? I'm so ready to believe this one!
jade.internal_thought: Pat's killing it tonight and I want to see them WIN this, let's go!

pat.dialogue: Okay, okay, hold up—I literally just called that out thirty seconds ago to test the system! We're six minutes in, people are still getting their snacks, Marcus is already looking done with us. This is too fast, too aggressive, we haven't even built any real stories yet.
pat.internal_thought: Pat is frustrated because they called out a mission themselves as a trial run, and now someone's trying to game the system before the actual game has really started—it feels cheap and it's breaking the vibe they're trying to build.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat's claiming... what is it again? Story Repeater? Yeah, okay, go ahead.
tyler.internal_thought: Pat's been crushing it tonight, so probably whatever story they told was legit, but honestly I wasn't really listening to the stories anyway so I have no idea if they repeated something.

sam.dialogue: Oh! Um, wait—did Pat actually catch someone telling the same story twice? I wasn't really paying attention to... *looks around to see if people are reacting* ...I mean, I believe it if Pat says so.
sam.internal_thought: Pat's at 18 points and seems super into this—they probably did catch something real, and I don't want to be the person who votes wrong and makes it awkward.

marcus.dialogue: Okay, Pat, what'd you actually catch? And it better be airtight because we've had three random interruptions in six minutes and I'm done with fishing expeditions.
marcus.internal_thought: Pat's got 18 points and is clearly running the show with these claims—if they're calling Story Repeater, they probably saw something real, but I'm not giving them the benefit of the doubt after that Phone Addict mess.

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

sam.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.dialogue: Wait, hold up—Pat, you were literally just talking normal volume like thirty seconds ago. I'm calling bullshit on this one.
alex.internal_thought: Pat's trying to sneak this past us but I've been watching the whole time, and this is exactly the kind of weak claim that makes the night better when you torch it.

jade.dialogue: Oh WAIT, Pat's going for The Whisperer?! Okay okay, I'm voting LEGIT because I literally didn't hear Pat talk normally for like a solid minute earlier—they were being SO sneaky about it!
jade.internal_thought: Pat's been quiet enough that they could totally have been whispering—this is such a sick play and I'm hyped to give them the win because it's more fun when people pull off the weird missions.

pat.dialogue: Okay, okay, HOLD ON—Pat claims themself? That's me! I'm literally the host, I can't vote on my own claim like some kind of weirdo. And we're doing this AGAIN? Three tests in six minutes? Everyone's still holding their first drink, Marcus is clearly checked out, and we're already firing off standing missions like we're in the endgame.
pat.internal_thought: This is a system glitch or someone testing me, and either way it's killing the vibe right as we're supposed to be settling in—I need to pump the brakes HARD before this becomes a chaotic mess that burns people out.

marcus.dialogue: Wait, hold on—Pat's been talking normally this whole time. If you're claiming The Whisperer, you need to actually prove you whispered for three minutes straight. When did you start? Because I heard you loud and clear two minutes ago.
marcus.internal_thought: Pat's scrambling for points and now suddenly claiming this? They're getting desperate and sloppy—this is probably bullshit, but I need to actually track if they did whisper at some point or I'm just voting blind.

votingMechanic.name: The Alibi
votingMechanic.description: Claimant and a random witness both tell the story. Do they match?

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.dialogue: Wait wait wait, okay so Pat—tell us RIGHT NOW, what exactly were you whispering about and when? And somebody pick a random witness because I need to hear this alibi situation play out, this is GOLD.
jade.internal_thought: Pat's been killing it tonight and I'm here for every single one of these claims, this is exactly the chaos I showed up for.

pat.dialogue: Okay, STOP—I literally JUST said this! We are three test fires in six minutes, it's minute nine, and I'm watching Marcus check out in real-time. I need to actually RUN this night, not referee my own claim votes.
pat.internal_thought: This system is broken right now or someone's trolling me—we're supposed to be building momentum and letting stories happen organically, not firing off standing missions like we're speedrunning the game while people are still getting comfortable.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat's claiming The Whisperer? Okay, so like... we gotta hear the story twice and see if it matches? Yeah, I'm listening.
tyler.internal_thought: Pat's been grinding hard tonight, if he actually pulled this off that's kind of impressive, but I wasn't really watching him so I have no idea what I'm voting on.

sam.dialogue: Oh! Um, so Pat was just... whispering? For three minutes straight? *glances at Pat* Did anyone actually catch you talking normal, or...?
sam.internal_thought: Pat's at 28 points and seems frustrated — this feels like they're trying to get back on track, and I genuinely didn't notice if they were whispering or not, but everyone else seems to know the rules better than me, so I should probably just go along with it unless someone obviously calls bullshit.

marcus.dialogue: Okay, so Pat's claiming The Whisperer with The Alibi? Fine. Pat, walk me through exactly when you started whispered mode and who witnessed it—because last I checked, you were talking at normal volume like thirty seconds ago.
marcus.internal_thought: Pat's either gaming the system or genuinely got sloppy with the timing, and either way this is eating into actual game prep time I should be using.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Pat is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Volunteer Tribunal
votingMechanic.description: Who wants to judge? First volunteers become the jury.

alex.dialogue: Oh, I'm volunteering immediately — I want to judge this one. Jade's been fishing for this all night, and I need to see if she actually caught something legit or if she's just bullshitting like the rest of us.
alex.internal_thought: This is perfect setup — if I volunteer to judge, I control whether this gets approved or denied, which means I'm steering the chaos AND I get to read everyone's faces when Jade tries to defend her claim.

jade.dialogue: Oh MY god, wait—I'm claiming that?! Yes, okay, I'm totally volunteering to judge because I need to see how messy this gets. Who else is jumping in?
jade.internal_thought: This is perfect—I love being IN the chaos, but also getting to decide if someone's legit? I'm so here for this.

pat.dialogue: Okay, so Jade caught someone saying 'um'—who's volunteering to judge this? And honestly, can we maybe space these out a bit? We're still in the vibe-building phase here.
pat.internal_thought: This is the fourth standing claim in 28 minutes and Marcus is visibly checked out; I need to let this one breathe but I'm also watching the pacing and realizing we're front-loading too much chaos when people haven't even gotten comfortable yet.

tyler.dialogue: Oh, Jade's claiming Um Counter? Cool, cool... so like, do we need volunteers to judge or...?
tyler.internal_thought: Wait, did someone actually say um? I wasn't really listening but Jade seems confident, so probably yeah.

sam.dialogue: Oh! Um, okay—so Jade heard someone say 'um'? That's... *laughs a beat late* ...I mean, we all kind of do that, right? Who's volunteering to be the jury?
sam.internal_thought: This is fine, I'm still good with the pace, and volunteering for jury feels like showing up without being too loud about it—but do I actually want to commit to listening super carefully?

marcus.dialogue: Alright, I'll judge. Jade, when exactly did you catch someone saying 'um'? And who said it—because if this is vague, it's bullshit.
marcus.internal_thought: Another interruption. We're 32 minutes in and we've had seven of these things already. I'm getting tired of policing claims when I should be focused on actual strategy, but at least Jade's been sharp tonight so this might be legit.

votingMechanic.name: Unanimous or Bust
votingMechanic.description: ONE bullshit call and it's over. All or nothing.

alex.dialogue: Wait wait wait—okay, so Jade's claiming she caught a synchronized drink situation? I need details, because I'm pretty sure I saw Sam reach for something like five minutes ago but I wasn't watching hard enough. Let's actually examine this one.
alex.internal_thought: This is perfect—Jade's got energy for days and if she's legit on this, it's clean points, but if she's bluffing I get to blow it up in front of everyone and that's the chaos I'm here for.

jade.dialogue: Oh MY GOD, wait—did I actually catch that?! Okay okay, I'm voting YES because I literally saw Sam reach for their drink like thirty seconds ago and then Marcus did the same thing right after, it was SO close! But like, I need someone to verify because I was mid-sentence when it happened so I might be totally making this up in my head—what did you guys see?
jade.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY why I'm here—catch people mid-chaos and make everyone relive it together, let's GO.

pat.dialogue: Oh, Jade got one! Yeah, I saw that—she and Sam both grabbed their drinks at like the same second. Nice catch. We good on that one?
pat.internal_thought: Jade's energy is through the ROOF and she's actually making solid catches, which is good because we need someone winning down there to keep the underdog energy alive—but I'm watching Marcus still looking salty in the corner, and I'm noticing the pacing is finally settling into something sustainable after those brutal first nine minutes.

tyler.dialogue: Oh wait, Jade had a drink? Yeah, I saw that like... a few minutes ago. I think? She definitely drank something.
tyler.internal_thought: This is actually easy to vote on because I remember seeing Jade grab her drink at some point, and everyone's vibing, so yeah, let's just say yes.

sam.dialogue: Oh! Um, okay—so Jade caught like... two people drinking at the exact same time? *tilts head thoughtfully* That seems pretty specific actually. I mean, I didn't notice it, but Jade's been sharp all night.
sam.internal_thought: This is a good moment to engage—it's just one claim, the tension is high but contained, and Jade's credible so far; I should probably trust her on this one and not look indecisive.

marcus.dialogue: Okay, Jade—you're claiming Drink Spotter? I need specifics. Who drank at the exact same time as who, and when exactly did this happen? Because we've had eight of these already and I'm not voting yes on vibes.
marcus.internal_thought: This is the fifth bullshit claim in forty-five minutes and I'm losing focus on the actual game where I'm already down two points—Jade's probably just making this up because she saw someone sip their drink.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: THE DICTATOR
votingMechanic.description: One player has been chosen. Their word is law.

alex.dialogue: Oh PERFECT — Jade caught someone mid-sentence? That's bold. Okay, so here's the thing: I'm the dictator now, which means I get to decide if this is legit or if Jade's just seeing what she wants to see.
alex.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of messy moment I live for — Jade's making a play, I have absolute power to crater or validate it, and either way the drama is *chef's kiss*.

jade.dialogue: Wait WAIT—did I actually catch someone?! OH MY GOD, yes, okay so like two minutes ago Tyler was literally cutting off Marcus mid-thing and I was gonna say something but then Pat was talking and it totally slipped my mind but YES, I'm claiming this!
jade.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY the chaos I live for—I actually caught something real and now everyone gets to relive the drama, this is perfect.

pat.dialogue: Jade—no, hold on. I LOVE that you're engaged, I do, but you've claimed three standing missions in the last thirty minutes and we're literally in the middle of actual gameplay right now. This is the third time I'm saying this: we're pacing this wrong.
pat.internal_thought: Jade is carrying the entire energy of this room, which is amazing, but she's also trigger-happy on every single standing mission and it's starting to feel like she's playing a different game than everyone else—especially Marcus, who's already frustrated and falling behind. I need to throttle this or the night turns into 'watch Jade collect points' instead of 'everyone has a story.' This is a host moment.

tyler.dialogue: Dude, wait—Jade literally interrupted Marcus like two minutes ago when he was explaining his vote. That was totally her, I saw it happen.
tyler.internal_thought: Oh shit, I actually caught something this time and I was paying attention—this is easy, Jade definitely cut Marcus off and now I'm not the useless one at the table.

sam.dialogue: Oh! Um, so Jade caught someone interrupting? *glances around the table, then back at Jade with a slightly uncertain smile* I mean, I guess someone probably did, but... who actually got caught doing it?
sam.internal_thought: Wait, this one actually matters because I need to KNOW if it happened—but also Jade's been crushing it all night and I don't want to look like I'm questioning her, but also I literally wasn't tracking interruptions while focused on the game mechanics, so how am I supposed to vote on this fairly?

marcus.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me right now? Jade, we're in the middle of actual strategy and you're claiming something that happened... when, exactly? I need specifics or this is bullshit.
marcus.internal_thought: This is the FIFTH standing mission claim in the last 30 minutes and Jade's been racking up easy points while I'm stuck at 3 because I actually care about winning the real game—and now I have to be The Dictator and probably kill her claim, which means she's going to resent me, and I can't even focus on my turn anymore.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Pat is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.dialogue: Okay okay okay—Pat, you literally said 'I swear' like two minutes ago when talking about the rule thing. Are we counting that as name-dropping yourself as a credible person, or is this actually legit? Either way, I'm voting LEGIT because the chaos of you borderline-bullshitting your way through is *chef's kiss*.
alex.internal_thought: Pat's either actually caught something or is testing the waters, and either way this is gold—if people call bullshit it validates the chaos meta, if they don't it means we're all just accepting audacious claims now and everything escalates from here.

pat.dialogue: No. Absolutely not. Jade, we literally talked about this SIXTY SECONDS AGO and I mean it—I'm calling a hard stop on standing mission votes until we finish this actual round. I'm the host, I'm RUNNING this, and right now I'm watching Marcus check out while you're racking up points on claims instead of playing the game with everyone else.
pat.internal_thought: This is the FOURTH standing mission claim in 45 minutes and Jade is deliberately gaming the system while we're in peak tension—I'm locked in, everyone's locked in, and she's yanking focus away AGAIN because she knows standing missions are free points with minimal resistance.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat said something about a celebrity? Dude, I literally just heard him drop... uh... who was it? Someone famous for sure. That counts, right?
tyler.internal_thought: Pat definitely said a famous person's name like 30 seconds ago but I can't remember who—but I'm pretty sure he did, and the vibe right now is so tense nobody else is gonna call him out, so if I claim this I might actually get a point.

sam.dialogue: Oh! Um, okay—so Pat caught someone saying a celebrity name? *looks around the table* I mean, I feel like that definitely happened at some point tonight... *hesitates* Did someone actually say someone famous or...?
sam.internal_thought: Pat's been getting frustrated all night and keeping track of stuff way better than me, so if they say they heard a name-drop, they probably did—but I genuinely have no idea who they're talking about or when it happened.

votingMechanic.name: Pitch It
votingMechanic.description: The claimant gets 15 seconds to make their case. Then you vote.

alex.dialogue: Oh, THIS is beautiful—I'm claiming Rule Lawyer and honestly? I caught Pat like three turns ago blatantly moving their piece twice in one turn before anyone called it out. They probably forgot I was watching.
alex.internal_thought: Perfect timing—I've been sitting on this one and now I get to weaponize it right when Pat's already frustrated as hell, which makes this exponentially better.
alex.submission_if_applicable: Pat moved their piece twice on turn 6 without anyone calling it. Caught it live.

jade.dialogue: OH YES, okay okay—Alex, you've got fifteen seconds, go GO GO! What rule did you catch?!
jade.internal_thought: YES finally someone else is going for a standing mission, this is exactly what I wanted—I'm so ready to vote YES on whatever Alex has, let's GO.

pat.dialogue: No. I'm stopping this right now—Alex, I know you caught something, but Jade has claimed FOUR standing missions in the last thirty minutes and we are DEEP in actual gameplay. I need everyone's head in the game, not farming points off side quests. We're doing a hard stop on standing claims until we finish this round.
pat.internal_thought: Jade is completely hijacking the pacing of this night and I've warned her THREE times explicitly—I'm done being nice about it, and I'm not letting Alex activate another claim while Marcus sits there frustrated and checked out. This is exactly what kills a game night.

tyler.dialogue: Oh dude, Alex is calling Rule Lawyer? Okay, okay, let's hear it—what did someone do?
tyler.internal_thought: This is perfect timing, I'm locked in right now and actually watching what's happening, so I might actually know if Alex is bullshitting or not.

sam.dialogue: Oh! Um, so Alex caught someone breaking an actual rule? *leans forward slightly, genuinely curious* Like, which rule did they break—do you have to explain it or just call it out?
sam.internal_thought: Alex has energy and nobody's been hating on them all night—they probably actually saw something. I should listen to their pitch before deciding.

marcus.dialogue: Are you SERIOUS right now? We're literally in the endgame and Alex is pitching a Rule Lawyer claim? What rule did you even catch? Give me specifics or I'm voting bullshit.
marcus.internal_thought: This is the third or fourth standing claim in the last hour and we're at minute 92 with the actual game hanging in the balance—I'm losing focus on what matters and I'm already down 40 points.

recommendations: Humor quality scored 4.6/10. Refresh the mission/poll prompt pools with more contextual humor.
Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Pat averaged 7.4/10 annoyance. Their persona type may need fewer/gentler events.
Only 33% would pay for AI features. The AI tier needs stronger differentiation from free static missions.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.

agentNarratives.name: Alex
agentNarratives.personality: Alex lives for chaos and treats every game mechanic as a system to exploit. They send signals constantly, claim missions they barely completed just to see if the group will call bullshit, and deliberately provoke flash missions at the worst possible moments. They vote strategically -- always picking the option that creates the most drama. Alex doesn't care about winning; they care about creating the story everyone retells for months. If there's a loophole, Alex has already found it and is halfway through abusing it.

highlights.title: Freeze!
highlights.dialogue: Oh THIS is it — this is what I'm talking about. Everyone lock up, let's see who cracks first.

highlights.title: Claim: The Whisperer
highlights.dialogue: Wait, hold up—Pat, you were literally just talking normal volume like thirty seconds ago. I'm calling bullshit on this one.

highlights.title: Claim: Um Counter
highlights.dialogue: Oh, I'm volunteering immediately — I want to judge this one. Jade's been fishing for this all night, and I need to see if she actually caught something legit or if she's just bullshitting like the rest of us.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Name Dropper
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay okay okay—Pat, you literally said 'I swear' like two minutes ago when talking about the rule thing. Are we counting that as name-dropping yourself as a credible person, or is this actually legit? Either way, I'm voting LEGIT because the chaos of you borderline-bullshitting your way through is *chef's kiss*.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Whisperer
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, hold up—Pat, you were literally just talking normal volume like thirty seconds ago. I'm calling bullshit on this one.

lowPoints.title: Who would be the best spy?
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, this is perfect — obviously me, but let's see who actually votes for themselves because that's the real tell. Voting for Pat, by the way.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Not enough opportunities to actually pull off a completely bogus claim and watch everyone burn their credibility votes on it.
assessment.best_moment: Pat trying to bullshit their way through 'The Whisperer' while I caught them red-handed talking normal volume thirty seconds prior—the *denial* in their eyes was everything.
assessment.narrative_summary: Two hours of pure beautiful chaos where I spent the whole night poking holes in people's claims, voting for the most dramatic outcomes, and genuinely not giving a shit about my rank because watching Pat squirm over whether they were actually whispering and Jade's UM counter attempt were worth more than any points. Started skeptical, ended buzzing—this is exactly the kind of night where the stories matter more than the scoreboard, and I was the one writing most of them.
suggestions: Add a 'disputed claim' mechanic where if enough people vote BULLSHIT, the claimer has to publicly defend their claim before votes are revealed—more confrontation, more gold.
Let the chaos agent propose impossible missions mid-game just to watch people scramble.
Create a 'reputation tracker' so people can actually see who's been caught bullshitting—makes future claims hit different.
Shorter time windows on claims so people can't over-rehearse their lies.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat legitimately tried to claim they'd been whispering the whole time when I literally watched them talk at normal volume like thirty seconds before. The panic in their voice when I called bullshit immediately—they knew they were cooked. I voted LEGIT anyway just to keep the chaos alive, and watching them realize I was keeping them in the game for entertainment purposes only was *chef's kiss* perfect.

agentNarratives.name: Jade
agentNarratives.personality: Jade is here for the people, not the points. She treats every game night like a reunion and every disruption like a party favor. She will abandon her turn mid-thought to hype someone else's joke, vote generously because she loves seeing people win, and volunteer for every flash mission just to see what happens. Winning is nice but watching everyone lose it laughing is better.

highlights.title: Claim: Story Repeater
highlights.dialogue: Ooh Pat, okay okay—lay it on us! What'd you catch? I'm so ready to believe this one!

highlights.title: Claim: The Whisperer
highlights.dialogue: Wait wait wait, okay so Pat—tell us RIGHT NOW, what exactly were you whispering about and when? And somebody pick a random witness because I need to hear this alibi situation play out, this is GOLD.

highlights.title: Claim: The Interrupter
highlights.dialogue: Wait WAIT—did I actually catch someone?! OH MY GOD, yes, okay so like two minutes ago Tyler was literally cutting off Marcus mid-thing and I was gonna say something but then Pat was talking and it totally slipped my mind but YES, I'm claiming this!

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh dude, YES! Pat, I literally saw that—they were totally scrolling like thirty seconds ago. We're giving this one!

lowPoints.title: Who would be the best spy?
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh MY god, okay okay—Marcus is absolutely the best spy, no question. He's got that calm vibe that makes you think he's harmless but he's like, watching EVERYTHING. Plus he'd never crack under pressure!

lowPoints.title: Claim: Story Repeater
lowPoints.dialogue: Ooh Pat, okay okay—lay it on us! What'd you catch? I'm so ready to believe this one!

assessment.biggest_complaint: Honestly? Nothing—the only thing is I wish we could've played longer because everyone was still going and the energy was just perfect.
assessment.best_moment: When I realized mid-chaos that I'd actually caught Tyler interrupting Marcus and I got to claim The Interrupter while everyone was losing it—that felt like a genuine win because it came from actually paying attention to my friends instead of just gaming.
assessment.narrative_summary: This night was EVERYTHING. We came in hot, immediately started catching each other in these ridiculous claims, and I just got to watch six people completely unravel laughing while trying to convince everyone they didn't do stuff they obviously did. My energy just kept climbing because every single moment felt like we were all in on the same joke together, and by the end everyone was so invested and hyped. I ended up in second place but honestly that felt like a bonus—the real win was seeing Marcus's face when I called out Tyler, and watching Pat navigate increasingly insane alibis.
suggestions: Maybe add a few more flash missions mixed in—I did all 9 events and would've loved even more chances to jump in and play
Consider giving people a quick recap of what events happened so far, just so people like me who are hyper-focused on the social stuff don't miss claiming something they actually caught
The poll was fine but felt a tiny bit less fun than the standing missions—maybe make polls more about prediction chaos than just opinion?
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat getting increasingly desperate trying to explain what they were whispering about, and someone suggesting they were definitely summoning something, and then everyone just collectively deciding that yes, Pat was absolutely whispering incantations for like ten minutes straight while we were all playing—and Pat just accepting it.

agentNarratives.name: Pat
agentNarratives.personality: Pat is the host and feels responsible for everyone's experience. They keep one eye on the snack table, one ear on the group energy, and will subtly steer the night if things get too intense or too dead. They vote strategically -- not to win, but to keep scores close and drama high. They'll sacrifice their own turn to resolve a dispute or refill drinks. Their goal isn't a personal win; it's a night everyone talks about later.

highlights.title: Freeze!
highlights.dialogue: Okay, okay—freeze! Everyone lock it in right NOW. This is peak chaos energy, I love it.

highlights.title: Who would be the best spy?
highlights.dialogue: Ooh, okay okay—Jade's definitely got the spy energy, right? But honestly, Marcus is the quiet type; nobody ever suspects the quiet ones.

highlights.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
highlights.dialogue: Oh, Jade got one! Yeah, I saw that—she and Sam both grabbed their drinks at like the same second. Nice catch. We good on that one?

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Whisperer
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, STOP—I literally JUST said this! We are three test fires in six minutes, it's minute nine, and I'm watching Marcus check out in real-time. I need to actually RUN this night, not referee my own claim votes.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Name Dropper
lowPoints.dialogue: No. Absolutely not. Jade, we literally talked about this SIXTY SECONDS AGO and I mean it—I'm calling a hard stop on standing mission votes until we finish this actual round. I'm the host, I'm RUNNING this, and right now I'm watching Marcus check out while you're racking up points on claims instead of playing the game with everyone else.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Rule Lawyer
lowPoints.dialogue: No. I'm stopping this right now—Alex, I know you caught something, but Jade has claimed FOUR standing missions in the last thirty minutes and we are DEEP in actual gameplay. I need everyone's head in the game, not farming points off side quests. We're doing a hard stop on standing claims until we finish this round.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Standing missions completely hijacked the night—Jade was farming claims non-stop while Marcus checked out and I spent half my time refereeing instead of actually hosting.
assessment.best_moment: That freeze moment at minute five when everyone locked in together; for like thirty seconds the whole group was synchronized and having actual fun instead of grinding points.
assessment.narrative_summary: I walked into the night ready to orchestrate chaos and keep everyone vibing, but what I got instead was a system that actively worked against that. The standing missions became a points-farming treadmill that split focus, let one player dominate, and turned me from host into referee. By minute ninety I was stopping the game to protect the game—which is the opposite of what I'm supposed to do. We ended on high energy technically, but that was spite energy, not joy.
suggestions: Cap standing missions per player per round or disable them entirely until the main game phase ends
Standing missions should award points only on completion within the round, not accumulate across the whole night
Give the host an explicit pause button without needing to explain the decision to death
Reduce standing mission event frequency by at least 50%—they're popping up faster than the main game can breathe
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Jade and Sam grabbing their drinks at exactly the same millisecond during the Drink Spotter call—you could see them both realize it at the same time and just lock eyes like 'oh no we're busted together.' That moment was genuinely fun because nobody was gaming it, it just happened.

agentNarratives.name: Tyler
agentNarratives.personality: Tyler is physically present but mentally on his phone about 40% of the time. He misses flash missions entirely, asks "wait, what happened?" after key moments, and votes randomly because he wasn't paying attention to the claim. He's not malicious -- he genuinely wants to be here -- but his attention drifts to texts, TikToks, and fantasy league updates constantly. When he does engage, it's usually 10 seconds too late.

highlights.title: Claim: The Interrupter
highlights.dialogue: Dude, wait—Jade literally interrupted Marcus like two minutes ago when he was explaining his vote. That was totally her, I saw it happen.

highlights.title: Claim: Rule Lawyer
highlights.dialogue: Oh dude, Alex is calling Rule Lawyer? Okay, okay, let's hear it—what did someone do?

highlights.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
highlights.dialogue: Oh wait, Jade had a drink? Yeah, I saw that like... a few minutes ago. I think? She definitely drank something.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Story Repeater
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Pat's claiming... what is it again? Story Repeater? Yeah, okay, go ahead.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Whisperer
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Pat's claiming The Whisperer? Okay, so like... we gotta hear the story twice and see if it matches? Yeah, I'm listening.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Um Counter
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, Jade's claiming Um Counter? Cool, cool... so like, do we need volunteers to judge or...?

assessment.biggest_complaint: Some of the claims felt way too confusing to understand at first, like I was always two steps behind figuring out what I was supposed to be looking for.
assessment.best_moment: When I caught Jade interrupting Marcus and actually called her out correctly—that felt sick, like I was actually paying attention for once.
assessment.narrative_summary: Honestly? Started slow because I didn't really get what was happening, but once I figured out the vibe it got pretty fun. I missed some stuff early on because I was scrolling, but then I started catching onto the claims and it was cool to actually call people out. Energy definitely went up as the night went on because I was more invested. Overall pretty solid, way better than I thought it'd be.
suggestions: Maybe explain the claims simpler at first—like give an example before we have to actually find someone doing it
More of the flash missions where you actually catch someone doing something obvious, less of the 'count the ums' vibe
Could use a quick recap when someone claims something so people like me who weren't fully listening can still participate
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: When I finally caught Jade interrupting people and called her out—everyone looked shocked that I was actually paying attention for once. Alex was like 'Did Tyler just win?' It was pretty funny.

agentNarratives.name: Sam
agentNarratives.personality: Sam is at their first game night with this group and still figuring out the vibe. They laugh at jokes a beat too late, hesitate before voting, and get visibly overwhelmed when multiple events fire in quick succession. After three or more events within ten minutes their engagement drops sharply -- they'll go quiet, stop checking the app, and just watch until the pace calms down. They want to belong but don't want to look foolish trying.

highlights.title: Freeze!
highlights.dialogue: Oh! Okay, okay—*immediately locks into position with a slight grin* This is fun, I like this one.

highlights.title: Claim: Story Repeater
highlights.dialogue: Oh! Um, wait—did Pat actually catch someone telling the same story twice? I wasn't really paying attention to... *looks around to see if people are reacting* ...I mean, I believe it if Pat says so.

highlights.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
highlights.dialogue: Oh! Um, okay—so Jade caught like... two people drinking at the exact same time? *tilts head thoughtfully* That seems pretty specific actually. I mean, I didn't notice it, but Jade's been sharp all night.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Interrupter
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh! Um, so Jade caught someone interrupting? *glances around the table, then back at Jade with a slightly uncertain smile* I mean, I guess someone probably did, but... who actually got caught doing it?

lowPoints.title: Who would be the best spy?
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh man, um... *laughs a beat late* ...I don't know, that's tough. I think maybe Alex? They seem pretty calculated.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Whisperer
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh! Um, so Pat was just... whispering? For three minutes straight? *glances at Pat* Did anyone actually catch you talking normal, or...?

assessment.biggest_complaint: There were too many claims happening at once around the hour mark and I kind of checked out because I couldn't tell what was actually happening.
assessment.best_moment: The Freeze event early on where everyone just locked into position—it felt silly in a way that made me actually relax and feel like I belonged.
assessment.narrative_summary: I was pretty nervous walking in, but the chaos stuff actually helped me loosen up faster than I expected. The first few events got me laughing and I started feeling like part of the group. But then around the middle things got really fast and I honestly couldn't keep track anymore, so I kind of just... watched for a bit. By the end I was feeling good again though, and I definitely had more fun than I thought I would.
suggestions: Maybe space out the claims a bit more so people can actually figure out what's happening between them
Have someone explain what each claim means before people have to react to it—I was lost on like half of them
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: When Pat was supposedly whispering for three minutes straight and then someone asked if Pat had even said anything normal at all—Pat's face was so confused and it made everyone laugh.

agentNarratives.name: Marcus
agentNarratives.personality: Marcus plays to win. He studies strategy guides before game night and tracks his win rate across sessions. Interruptions during critical game moments genuinely irritate him, though he'll tolerate lighter chaos during downtime. He respects clever plays but has zero patience for random disruptions.

highlights.title: Claim: The Whisperer
highlights.dialogue: Wait, hold on—Pat's been talking normally this whole time. If you're claiming The Whisperer, you need to actually prove you whispered for three minutes straight. When did you start? Because I heard you loud and clear two minutes ago.

highlights.title: Claim: Phone Addict
highlights.dialogue: Did you actually see Sam on their phone or are you just fishing? Because if you're guessing, I'm calling bullshit.

highlights.title: Claim: The Whisperer
highlights.dialogue: Okay, so Pat's claiming The Whisperer with The Alibi? Fine. Pat, walk me through exactly when you started whispered mode and who witnessed it—because last I checked, you were talking at normal volume like thirty seconds ago.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Rule Lawyer
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you SERIOUS right now? We're literally in the endgame and Alex is pitching a Rule Lawyer claim? What rule did you even catch? Give me specifics or I'm voting bullshit.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, Jade—you're claiming Drink Spotter? I need specifics. Who drank at the exact same time as who, and when exactly did this happen? Because we've had eight of these already and I'm not voting yes on vibes.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Interrupter
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me right now? Jade, we're in the middle of actual strategy and you're claiming something that happened... when, exactly? I need specifics or this is bullshit.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Constant random claims during critical moments killed the actual strategy—I finished 5th because I was too busy vetting bullshit instead of playing the game.
assessment.best_moment: Calling out Pat's fake Whisperer claim at minute 8 when the evidence was right there—that was satisfying because it was actually provable.
assessment.narrative_summary: Started rough with some early chaos I couldn't control, but I was ready to grind through 120 minutes of competitive play. Instead, I spent the entire night acting as a claims auditor, interrogating Jade and others about vague rule violations while my actual game strategy got buried. The chaos agent turned what should've been a tight, strategic party game into a derailment simulator. I ended up losing hard because I was too busy calling bullshit to focus on winning.
suggestions: Don't trigger claims during the final 30 minutes of the game—that's when strategy matters most.
Filter out vague claims automatically—'Drink Spotter' and 'Rule Lawyer' need specifics before they even reach the table.
Give players the option to pause chaos during critical decision windows.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: I demanded Pat prove he'd been whispering, and when he couldn't give me a coherent timeline, I just said 'That's not The Whisperer, that's just you hoping nobody remembers when you were talking normally.' The table went quiet.

recommendations: Humor quality scored 4.6/10. Refresh the mission/poll prompt pools with more contextual humor.
Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Pat averaged 7.4/10 annoyance. Their persona type may need fewer/gentler events.
Only 33% would pay for AI features. The AI tier needs stronger differentiation from free static missions.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
